title: The Further Adventures: Planet of the Hoojibs
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{Audiobook |image=[[File:Planetofthehoojibs.jpg]] |title=''The Further Adventures: Planet of the Hoojibs'' |author=*[[David Michelinie]]
{{C|original comic}}{{SW|url=news/star-wars-in-the-uk-read-along-adventures|text=''Star Wars'' in the UK: Read-Along Adventures}} *[[Jymn Magon]] {{C|adaptation}}{{SW|url=news/6-vintage-star-wars-picture-books|text=6 Vintage ''Star Wars'' Picture Books}} |narrator= |producer=[[Ted Kryczko]]{{WebCite|url=https://www.discogs.com/release/1108621-No-Artist-Star-Wars-The-Further-Adventures-Planet-Of-The-Hoojibs|text=Star Wars: The Further Adventures - Planet Of The Hoojibs|archivedate=20230416062603}} |director= |cover artist=[[Greg Winters]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-in-the-uk-read-along-adventures|text=''Star Wars'' in the UK: Read-Along Adventures}} |illustrator= |publisher=[[Buena Vista Records]] |release date=[[1983]] |rereleased= |media type=*[[Wikipedia:Phonograph record|7" 33 ⅓ rpm record]] {{C|454}} *[[Wikipedia:Cassette tape|Cassette tape]] {{C|154DC}} |run time=10 minutes''[[The Further Adventures: Planet of the Hoojibs]]'' |discs=1 |cassettes=1 |pages=24 |isbn=none |timeline=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]The story occurs short after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] which the ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] |series=''[[Star Wars: The Further Adventures]]'' |preceded by=[[The Further Adventures: Droid World|''Droid World'']] |followed by= }} {{Quote|We were forced out of our home by a b-b-beast!|Plif|}} '''''The Further Adventures: Planet of the Hoojibs''''' was a book-and-record in the ''[[Star Wars: The Further Adventures]]'' series. It was an adaptation of [[Star Wars (1977) 55|''Star Wars'' (1977) 55]] by [[David Michelinie]]. The book was adapated by [[Jymn Magon]] as part of [[Buena Vista Records]]' [[Read-Along Adventures]] and featured new artwork by [[Greg Winters]]. It was produced by [[Ted Kryczko]] and released in [[1983]] as book with [[Wikipedia:Phonograph record|7" 33 ⅓ rpm record]] and [[Wikipedia:Cassette tape|Cassette tape]] It is unknown who narrated the record as credits were not provided along with the book or record. ==Plot summary== [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] leads a survey team to the forest [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Arbra/Legends|Arbra]] to determine if the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] could establish a permanent base after they had been forced to evacuate their previous outpost on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. Leia's crew discover that the woods are inhabited by small, telepathic, rodent-like creatures known as [[Hoojib/Legends|Hoojibs]]. The Hoojibs feed off electricity, so during the evening, they scamper around the survey camp, draining all of the power away from the Rebels' equipment and weaponry. The following morning, Princess Leia encounters a Hoojib named [[Plif/Legends|Plif]]—the [[spokesmind]] for the entire Hoojib population. Plif explains that the Hoojibs thrive off electrical radiation and that they typically feed from a trove of geothermal [[Energy crystal|crystals]] located in a nearby cave. However, they had recently been forced to abandon the cave due to the presence of a large, flying creature known as a [[Slivilith]]. Leia agrees to help Plif oust the Slivilith so that the Hoojibs can regain their food source and stop draining resources from the Rebels. The Slivilith flies downward into the survey camp and begins attacking the Rebels. The officers are practically powerless due to the fact that their [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] no longer hold a charge. They herd the creature back towards its nesting grotto and keep it at bay by way of non-energy based melee weapons such as lances and pole arms. [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] manages to grab the Slivilith by its tentacles and smash it down upon the power rod crystals, killing it. Grateful for their assistance, Plif volunteers to share the environs of Arbra with the Rebel Alliance. Leia reports back to the fleet that they have found a new permanent base of operations. Meanwhile, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] has a conversation with [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]. Lando has had concerns about the state of affairs in [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] on the planet [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] ever since he voluntarily abdicated his position as Baron Administrator. Taking the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], Lando excuses himself from the fleet and returns to Cloud City. ==Continuity== ''Planet of the Hoojibs'' presents some events slightly differently than ''Star Wars'' 55 in a somewhat simplified story. In ''Planet of the Hoojibs'', the Hoojibs actually speak instead of communicating telepathically as in ''Star Wars'' 55 In addition, Luke Skywalker and Lando Calrissian never appear in this version of the story. ==Media== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from The Further Adventures: Planet of the Hoojibs}} ===Editions=== *[[1983]]; Buena Vista Records; Vinyl LP record *1983; Buena Vista Records;
